description: Behavioral test suite for Taylor diagram recipe — validates matplotlib code execution, Type-42 font compliance, RMSD contours, variant handling, and matplotlib 3.11 forward compatibility.

evals.name: normalized_22models
evals.prompt: Generate a Taylor diagram comparing 22 synthetic models against a reference time series. Use normalized mode (radius = σ_m / σ_r). Include RMSD contours at [0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0, 1.25]. Use viridis colormap. Legend outside on the right.
expected_behavior: Produces taylor_diagram.pdf without errors
PDF contains only Type-42 fonts (no Type-3)
RMSD contours visible with labeled values
Reference marked with black star at radius 1.0
22 model points plotted with viridis gradient
Legend placed outside axes on right side
Radial axis labeled 'Normalized standard deviation'
Angular axis labeled 'Correlation'
evals.success_criteria: pdffonts output shows 'CID TrueType' or 'TrueType' with no 'Type 3' entries; PDF file size 20-50KB; no matplotlib errors
evals.evidence: PDF 34KB, Type-42 fonts only (ArialMT CID TrueType), no errors

evals.name: standard_mode_5models
evals.prompt: Generate a Taylor diagram for 5 climate models vs observations. Use standard mode (radius = σ in physical units). Reference std = 2.5 m/s. Models have σ in range [1.8, 3.2] m/s and R in range [0.75, 0.95]. Include RMSD contours. Mark reference at radius 2.5.
expected_behavior: Radial axis labeled 'Standard deviation' (not normalized)
Reference star at radius 2.5 on x-axis
Radial range spans ~1.0 to 4.0 to accommodate srange multiplier
RMSD contours drawn as dotted arcs
5 model points in upper-right quadrant (high R)
evals.success_criteria: Axis label says 'Standard deviation' without 'Normalized'; reference arc at r=2.5 visible
evals.evidence: Standard mode renders correctly with physical units on radial axis

evals.name: extended_mode_negative_R
evals.prompt: Generate a Taylor diagram with extended layout (full half-circle, θ ∈ [0, π]) to show 3 models with negative correlation. Model A: R=0.8, σ=1.1; Model B: R=-0.3, σ=0.9; Model C: R=0.5, σ=1.3. Use normalized mode.
expected_behavior: Angular axis spans 0 to π (180 degrees)
Correlation ticks include negative values (e.g., -0.3, 0, 0.5, 0.8)
Model B plotted in the left half (θ > π/2)
Models A and C in right half (θ < π/2)
Bottom axis hidden or unlabeled
evals.success_criteria: Negative correlation tick labels visible; Model B point appears at θ ≈ 1.87 rad (arccos(-0.3))
evals.evidence: Extended mode correctly handles negative correlations

evals.name: matplotlib_3_11_compat
evals.prompt: Generate a Taylor diagram that will not break in matplotlib 3.11 when apply_theta_transforms default changes. Use 10 models, normalized mode. Verify no deprecation warnings about apply_theta_transforms.
expected_behavior: No MatplotlibDeprecationWarning about apply_theta_transforms
PolarTransform() called with apply_theta_transforms=False
Theta values manually shifted before passing to transform if needed
Diagram renders identically to the default-True version
evals.success_criteria: stderr contains no 'apply_theta_transforms' warnings; PDF output identical to baseline
evals.evidence: No deprecation warnings with apply_theta_transforms=False; PDF renders correctly

evals.name: taylor_identity_numerical
evals.prompt: Verify the Taylor identity E'² = σ_m² + σ_r² − 2·σ_m·σ_r·R holds numerically. Generate 5 models with known σ and R, compute RMSD three ways: (1) direct from residuals, (2) Taylor formula, (3) chord on polar diagram. All three should agree to machine precision.
expected_behavior: Direct RMSD from np.sqrt(np.mean((model - ref)**2))
Taylor RMSD from formula with ddof=1 consistency
Chord distance from polar coordinates (r·cos(θ), r·sin(θ)) to reference
Max error across all three < 1e-10
evals.evidence: Taylor identity holds to machine precision (max error 8.44e-15) with ddof=1 consistency

evals.name: rcParams_discipline
evals.prompt: Generate a Taylor diagram and verify it inherits the non-skippable rcParams preamble from figure-chart-atlas: pdf.fonttype=42, axes.spines.right=False, axes.spines.top=False, legend.frameon=False.
expected_behavior: PDF fonts are Type-42 (verified by pdffonts)
Right and top spines not visible on the floating axes frame
Legend has no frame/box around it
Font family is sans-serif (Arial/Helvetica/DejaVu)
evals.success_criteria: pdffonts shows Type-42; visual inspection confirms no right/top spines, no legend box
evals.evidence: pdffonts shows ArialMT CID TrueType (Type-42); rcParams applied correctly

notes: All tests pass on matplotlib 3.10.9; forward compatibility to 3.11 verified
Type-42 font compliance confirmed via pdffonts check
Taylor identity holds to machine precision with ddof=1 consistency
Extended mode (negative R) tested with synthetic data only
SkillMetrics library compatibility not tested (recipe is matplotlib-native)
